cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully and try to find the answer to the question. The question is a multiple-choice inference question and the options are: (A) there are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar (B) the new coin can buy more than the old coin (C) the new coin takes on a white color (D) the Sacagawea Golden Dollar is bigger Let me read the passage again to find the relevant information. The passage says: "The Presidential 1 coins will be the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. However, there is an important difference. For the first time since the 1930s, there is an inscription on the edge of each coin." From this statement, we can infer that the Presidential 1 coins and the Sacagawea Golden Dollar are the same size and color. So, the new coins are gold-colored (same as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar). Now, let's analyze the options: Option A: "there are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar" The passage does not confirm or deny whether the Sacagawea Golden Dollar has words or not. It only states that the Presidential 1 coins have inscriptions on the edge for the first time since the 1930s. This doesn't necessarily mean the Sacagawea Golden Dollar has no words. This option is not supported by the passage. Option B: "the new coin can buy more than the old coin" The passage doesn't mention anything about the purchasing power of the coins. It is just talking about the design and historical significance of the coins, not their value. This option is not supported by the passage. Option C: "the new coin takes on a white color" The passage explicitly states that the Presidential 1 coins are gold-colored, same as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. So this option is directly contradicted by the passage. Option D: "the Sacagawea Golden Dollar is bigger" The passage states that the Presidential 1 coins are the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. So this option is also not correct. If they are the same size, then the Sacagawea Dollar is not bigger. Hmm, none of the options seem to be correct based on the passage. Let me double-check the passage for any other relevant information. Wait, the passage says "the Presidential 1 coins will be the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. However, there is an important difference. For the first time since the 1930s, there is an inscription on the edge of each coin." This means that the Sacagawea Golden Dollar does not have an inscription on the edge (otherwise it would have been mentioned as the same feature). However, this doesn't mean there are "no words" on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar - just that there are no inscriptions on the edge. Coins typically have inscriptions on the face and reverse. So option A is not correct. The passage only states that the Presidential 1 coins have the first edge inscriptions
